channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Netanyahu’s Reelection Could Hinge on Outcome of Iran War
item.description: He promised Israelis “total victory” but has fallen short on three fronts.

item.title: Trump’s Big Weekend Could Derail the G-7
item.description: With or without a deal, the Iran war looms large over the three-day leaders’ summit.

item.title: How South Africa’s Myopia Is Harming the Entire Continent
item.description: Xenophobia in Africa’s southernmost nation will have vast implications.

item.title: The Aliens Aren’t the Most Unbelievable Part of Steven Spielberg’s New Movie
item.description: Would even extraterrestrials inspire humanity to unite these days?

item.title: Russia, Land of the Unpredictable Past
item.description: A new censorship law restricts what Russians were ever allowed to have sung, written, or published.

item.title: What in the World?
item.description: Test yourself on the week of June 6: Peru’s presidential race heads for a photo finish, Xi Jinping visits North Korea, and the World Cup kicks off.

item.title: The Swiss would be foolish to cap their population at 10m
item.description: Immigration sometimes needs brakes, but the proposal would be like driving into a wall

item.title: Trump Is Shattering the Illusion of the West
item.description: The president’s turn to imperial civilizationalism is destroying what it claims to defend.

item.title: How Best to Look at Russian History
item.description: A new book shows the uses and limits of microhistories in understanding the past.

item.title: Will Farmers Lose Faith With Trump?
item.description: The economic effects of the Iran war are straining a key Republican constituency ahead of midterm elections.

item.title: Treating pancreatic tumours may have revealed cancer’s master switch
item.description: A promising drug could be the first of an entirely new class of treatments

item.title: Cover Story newsletter: A World Cup paradox
item.description: How we chose this week’s image

item.title: The value of SpaceX rockets on its stock-market debut
item.description: Elon Musk has become the world’s first trillionaire

item.title: Inequality in Évian
item.description: The G7 summit must recognize that extreme disparities in income, wealth, opportunity, and political influence are not only a defining feature of the global economy but also a major factor in many other shared challenges. For too long, the issue has been treated as a symptom rather than a cause.

item.title: Elon Musk’s Self-Contradictory Military Policy
item.description: The soon-to-be trillionaire’s politics undermine his own business as a government contractor.

item.title: Europeans Are the G7’s Grownups
item.description: Slowly but surely, the European Union is taking steps to reduce its current-account surplus and direct more of its substantial savings into domestic investment and growth. Unfortunately, at the G7 Summit in France this month, the United States will refuse to address global imbalances, including its own.

item.title: Missile Defense Worked Against Iran. It Might Not Work Against China.
item.description: The ongoing war has revealed vulnerabilities that could prove far more dangerous in a conflict over Taiwan.

item.title: Don’t Invite Syria Back Into Lebanon
item.description: Washington is contemplating the one thing that could rally Lebanese around Hezbollah.

item.title: Charting the Future of the Indonesian Military’s Involvement in Counterterrorism
item.description: While TNI’s past counterterrorism involvement has proved beneficial, it is important not to overlook how a further expansion of its role could complicate existing efforts.

item.title: Modi Is Rigging Indian Democracy
item.description: Under the guise of routine voter-roll maintenance, India’s Election Commission has purged tens of millions of voters, disproportionately affecting opposition strongholds, the poor, and Muslim minorities. The result is an electoral system increasingly designed to favor the ruling Bharatiya Janata Party.

item.title: Learning to Govern a Fragmented World
item.description: As the postwar multilateral order gives way to a multipolar and more contested world, global governance must adapt or risk irrelevance. The upcoming G7 summit offers a unique opportunity to champion a coalition-based approach that translates shared interests into collective action.

item.title: How Russia Could Escalate
item.description: With progress in Ukraine grinding to a halt, Moscow could seek disruption off the battlefield.

item.title: Cognition for Sale
item.description: AI is doing what no previous technology could, turning tacit human knowledge into a product that can be extracted, priced, and distributed at scale. Yet the same technology that democratizes cognitive labor may also weaken the human capacities on which complex societies depend.

item.title: As Cambodia Cracks Down, Cyberscam Networks Test Sri Lanka
item.description: What happens next will determine whether the island is a temporary refuge for foreign cybercriminals or becomes part of Asia’s cybercrime infrastructure.

item.title: Cambodia-Thailand Relations, Not Myanmar, Will Define ASEAN’s Immediate Future
item.description: The ongoing conflict between the two member states is a direct challenge to the Southeast Asian bloc's founding purpose.

item.title: Three Characteristics of China’s Criticism of the Takaichi Administration
item.description: Beijing’s campaign against Tokyo is unlike anything seen before.

item.title: Philippine Defense Secretary Vows to Defend Nation’s Interests After China Sanctions
item.description: The country's Department of Foreign Affairs said that the sanctions against Gilberto Teodoro Jr. are “an unfriendly act" that "further complicates" bilateral relations.

item.title: Has the Japan-DPRK Pyongyang Declaration Been Abandoned?
item.description: A quiet revision to Chongryon’s platform carries significant implications for Tokyo.

item.title: China’s Edifice Complex
item.description: Why Beijing can’t stop wasteful spending.

item.title: The Strange Defeat of Nuclear Deterrence
item.description: And the coming crisis in strategic stability.

item.title: Thai Court Sentences 2 Men to Death For Involvement in 2015 Erawan Shrine Bombing
item.description: The attack on the popular tourist attraction in central Bangkok killed 20 people and injured around 120 more.

item.title: Japan and South Korea Navigate a Turbulent World
item.description: Two neighbors, one alliance, and a great deal of uncertainty.

item.title: AUKUS After AUKMIN: The Test Beyond Washington
item.description: While the U.S. remains the dominant partner in AUKUS, closer collaboration between Canberra and London will be vital to its success.

item.title: What’s the Philippine Senate Drama All About?
item.description: The country's upper house has become highly polarized, creating doubts about the upcoming impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte.

item.title: The Yuan’s Quiet Advance on Commodity Pricing
item.description: A miner paid in yuan has a reason to borrow in yuan, and a miner indebted in yuan has a reason to price in it.

item.title: What Is the Threshold for Action?: A Question Afghanistan Asks the World
item.description: How long must the Taliban continue internal repression and support for terrorist groups before the international community recognizes the real scale of the threat?

item.title: Xi’s Summit with Kim: Views from the Neighborhood
item.description: By keeping neighbors guessing about his intentions and avoiding any concessions, it is Kim who appears to have gained the most from the Chinese leader's visit.

item.title: The Geopolitics of China-Israel Trade and Investment
item.description: Insights from Tomer Fadlon.

item.title: Explaining the Philippines’ Defeat in the UN Security Council Election
item.description: The country's loss to Kyrgyzstan points to shifting coalitions within the U.N. and exposes the limits of alliance politics in multilateral settings.

item.title: What to Make of the Emergence of a Russia-Taliban Security Alliance
item.description: While the agreement may serve the immediate security interests of both parties, it also has the potential to alter regional geopolitical dynamics.

item.title: China’s ‘Special Mission’ Aircraft Boom
item.description: The Chinese military is fielding an increasingly broad range of “special mission” aircraft, which have advanced in both quantity and quality over the past decade.

item.title: On America’s 250th, go back to “Democracy in America”
item.description: In praise of Alexis de Tocqueville

item.title: Forget the World Cup. Culture is becoming more fragmented
item.description: Farewell to the monoculture
